dependencies_on_other_workstreams: Gateway platform (GW-*): OAuth2 client-credentials issuance, Idempotency-Key 24h replay store, HMAC-SHA256 webhook signing (X-TEEP-Signature + X-TEEP-Timestamp), and RFC7807 problem+json error envelope must be live for per-system conformance (CUTOVER-4) and TaskHub-write soak (CUTOVER-9).

Bootstrap + Asset Registry workstream: signed S3 JSON drops shaped exactly like the future REST responses (CUTOVER-3), plus asset_metadata.{assets,aliases,bindings} resolving cross-system IDs before enrichment — go-live slices validate against the registry.
Sensirion API integration workstream: device poll cadence pinned and device->pad/well mapping delivered before the Sensirion go-live slice (CUTOVER-5).
FMP/TaskHub API workstream (Sebastian): the net-new read+write /v1/fmp/* endpoints + task.updated webhook must be deployed before the TaskHub-write soak (CUTOVER-9).
Cygnet-via-existing-intermediary workstream (Mike + Darko): the chosen intermediary path and the adapted Cygnet adapter must be in place before the Cygnet go-live slice (CUTOVER-6).
ProCount/Carte read workstream (IFS Merrick owner): endpoints live and the Carte-fold-in question resolved before CUTOVER-7.
Identity/SSO workstream (Sahir): Entra ID enterprise app registration, claims + group->role mapping, optional SCIM, and conditional-access review feed the SSO production-cutover gate (CUTOVER-13).
Bedrock workstream (Darko + Taikun): cross-account IAM, Claude model enablement in the TEEP region, and LiteLLM gateway repointing feed the Bedrock cutover gate (CUTOVER-14).

Reporting/UI workstream: Sierra's exact 22-column HSE/EPA export and the Integration Health screen markup (mockups-html/04-integration-health.html) are lit up against live metrics in CUTOVER-11 and validated in the rehearsal (CUTOVER-15) and weekly reviews (CUTOVER-17).

deliverables.name: Per-system go-live sequencing plan + RACI
deliverables.description: One-page plan fixing the go-live order (read systems first, TaskHub write last), the config-only base-URL flip mechanism, go/no-go gates per system, the email-fallback trigger, and the named go-live owner per slice.

deliverables.name: Gateway-pointed verification harness
deliverables.description: Parameterized api-tests/ suite (run-tests.sh + smoke.sh 17 endpoints + simulator.py 4 scenarios) that runs identically against Prism mock, bootstrap S3-drop data, and TEEP's real gateway by changing only --base-url and the injected OAuth2 token; emits per-endpoint per-target pass/fail.

deliverables.name: Per-system gateway conformance reports
deliverables.description: One report per system (Sensirion, Cygnet-via-intermediary, ProCount, Carte, TaskHub-read, TaskHub-write) proving status codes, RFC7807 error envelope, Idempotency-Key handling, and HMAC webhook verification against the real gateway, plus a defect log routed to GW-*.

deliverables.name: Per-system go-live sign-offs
deliverables.description: Sensirion (webhook + poll fallback), Cygnet-via-intermediary (signal fidelity vs the 4h pre-event window), ProCount/Carte (codes/comments/injection + Carte-fold-in decision), and TaskHub-write (zero-duplicate idempotency soak + webhook round-trip) go-live sign-offs validated against known Jan-2026 alerts.

deliverables.name: Config-cutover changelog + rollback procedure
deliverables.description: Per-system record of the bootstrap-to-gateway base-URL flip (config-only) with post-flip parity reports and a verified instant-rollback (revert config to the S3-drop source).

deliverables.name: Email-fallback path (verified) + per-environment toggle
deliverables.description: The TaskHub-write-slips contingency: agent emails MRO the full evidence pack instead of POSTing a task, config-toggled per environment, with the office-cleared auto-close path proven independent of write.

deliverables.name: Production monitoring stack + Integration Health screen
deliverables.description: Prometheus-to-CloudWatch metrics (teep_api_calls_total / latency / errors per system + FR-35 agent KPIs) feeding the live Integration Health screen, with metadata-only logging confirmed.

deliverables.name: Failure-notification runbook + verified alerting
deliverables.description: Wired and tested alerting on the TEEP-chosen channel (email default / Teams / PagerDuty) at the >=5-failures-in-5-min threshold, auth-failure rotate-retry-alert, and >15-min degraded-mode behavior (flag Undetected, notify MRO, keep dashboard up, never invent a classification).

deliverables.name: SSO + Bedrock production-cutover sign-offs
deliverables.description: Verified Entra ID corporate login with role-mapped views per persona (consuming the identity workstream) and verified Bedrock LLM inference with decision-parity + latency + residency (consuming the Bedrock workstream).

deliverables.name: Production-rehearsal report + go/no-go decision
deliverables.description: Full end-to-end rehearsal of all 4 scenarios against the real stack on replayed events, with Sierra confirming her 22 columns auto-populate, plus the recorded production go/no-go decision.

deliverables.name: Weekly mapping-review log + versioned mapping config
deliverables.description: Operate-window record of weekly reviews with Sierra adjusting the free-text-to-category mapping (as config, not code) and re-confirming the monthly export matches her 22-column template.

deliverables.name: 60-day operate report measured against the 6 success criteria
deliverables.description: KPI trend reports (MTTA, auto-close rate, classification accuracy, per-system API error rate) + incident log, assessed against the PRD's six pilot success criteria, with accumulated data for KPI recalibration.

deliverables.name: Pilot review readout + Phase-2 go/no-go + scope proposal
deliverables.description: End-of-pilot readout with Devin + Sierra written sign-off (criterion 6), a recommendation on moving the auto-close target toward 65%, and a scoped Phase-2 proposal.

risks.risk: Duplicate TaskHub dispatch task created on a write retry — a bug here puts a real, duplicate field job in front of a lease operator (physical-world consequence).
risks.mitigation: Gate TaskHub-write go-live (CUTOVER-9) behind a dedicated idempotency-replay soak: replay the same Idempotency-Key on simulated 5xx and prove zero duplicates across 50+ writes within the 24h replay window before enabling write in production; keep email-fallback armed as the alternative.

risks.risk: TaskHub write API (net-new build by Sebastian) slips past go-live, blocking the field-cleared 43.5% close-the-loop.
risks.mitigation: Arm the config-toggled email-fallback (CUTOVER-10) so the office-cleared 56.5% goes live on schedule and field events get the email path with the same evidence pack until write turns on; sequence TaskHub write last so it never blocks read-system go-live.

risks.risk: Cygnet intermediary path (FMP ~30-min poll / ProCount integration / historian) delivers SCADA signals at coarser cadence than the 4h pre-event window needs, weakening the pressure-drop signatures auto-close relies on and degrading classification accuracy below the >=92% target.
risks.mitigation: Validate signal fidelity against >=10 known Jan-2026 alerts during the Cygnet slice (CUTOVER-6); document achievable resolution; if too coarse, escalate intermediary-path choice to Mike + Darko and route ambiguous cases to Undetected->human rather than mis-auto-closing.

risks.risk: SSO (Sahir/TEEP IT change control) or Bedrock (TEEP AWS cross-account + model enablement) production gates slip, pushing full production cutover beyond the deck's aggressive 6-8 week aspiration.
risks.mitigation: Let read-system soak proceed against bootstrap/early-gateway data via the Taikun-managed login + default direct-Anthropic LLM path so the pilot clock can start; treat SSO and Bedrock as blocking only for full production cutover (CUTOVER-13/14), not for read-system verification; engage Sahir and Darko on lead times at kickoff.

risks.risk: An API incident in production forces Darko's team to roll back Taikun's access, failing success criterion 5 and ending the pilot.
risks.mitigation: Stand up monitoring + alerting (CUTOVER-11/12) and prove degraded-mode behavior before go-live; rate-limit writes to <=5/min; metadata-only logging; per-system config rollback to S3-drop source; immediate incident triage with a logged resolution path.

risks.risk: The 22-day January sample (168 alerts) is unrepresentative, so production KPIs (especially the 40% auto-close target) diverge from the modeled baseline.
risks.mitigation: Use the 60-day operate window as a data-collection window and combine with the 6-12 month historical pull to recalibrate targets with evidence before publishing; report KPI trends weekly so divergence is caught early.

risks.risk: Bootstrap S3-drop JSON shapes drift from the gateway's eventual responses, breaking the config-only cutover guarantee and forcing real adapter rework at cutover.
risks.mitigation: Run the bootstrap dry-run (CUTOVER-3) against S3-drop data and file any shape drift as a defect to the bootstrap workstream immediately; treat teep-api.yaml as the single frozen contract both sides validate against.

risks.risk: HSE/EPA category vocabulary drifts as new LO phrasings appear during the pilot, eroding classification accuracy and Sierra's report parity.
risks.mitigation: Weekly mapping-table review with Sierra (CUTOVER-17) capturing new phrasings as config changes (not code), with each cycle re-confirming the 22-column export matches her template.

risks.risk: IFS Merrick stack owner (ProCount/Carte) remains TBD, stalling the ProCount/Carte go-live slice and the Carte-fold-in decision.
risks.mitigation: Escalate owner identification at kickoff (cross-workstream open decision); meanwhile validate ProCount/Carte against bootstrap S3-drop data so the slice is ready the moment the owner and endpoints land.

open_decisions.question: Is the TaskHub write API (POST/PATCH /v1/fmp/tasks + task.updated webhook) confirmed ready by go-live, or do we launch on the email-fallback path?
open_decisions.recommended_default: Plan for email-fallback at go-live (config-toggled), then flip to TaskHub write the moment Sebastian's API passes the idempotency soak — this lets the office-cleared 56.5% launch on time regardless.
open_decisions.needed_by: Before the Cutover phase begins (gates CUTOVER-9 vs CUTOVER-10)

open_decisions.question: Which channel and which named contact should receive API failure notifications (email default, Teams, or PagerDuty/Opsgenie)?
open_decisions.recommended_default: Email to a named Darko/Mike distribution as the default; add Teams via an integration key if TEEP provides one (PRD Q7).
open_decisions.needed_by: Before CUTOVER-12 (failure-notification wiring), during the Build phase

open_decisions.question: Which existing intermediary pipe will source Cygnet SCADA signals (FMP/TaskHub ~30-min device poll, ProCount's documented Cygnet integration, or a historian/read-replica/curated export)?
open_decisions.recommended_default: Default to the ProCount documented Cygnet integration if it carries the needed tags at usable cadence; otherwise FMP's ~30-min poll, accepting reduced 4h-pre-event-window fidelity. Decide jointly with Mike + Darko.
open_decisions.needed_by: Before CUTOVER-6 (Cygnet go-live slice); ideally at kickoff since it shapes the Cygnet adapter

open_decisions.question: Who is the IFS Merrick stack owner at TEEP (ProCount + Carte), and can Carte's injection-rate data be served through the ProCount API so the separate Carte endpoint is dropped?
open_decisions.recommended_default: Name the production-accounting owner (not Michelle/Mike) at kickoff; fold Carte into ProCount if injection data is exposed there, dropping the separate Carte slice (PRD Q-IFS).

open_decisions.question: What is the TEEP AWS account number and the Bedrock region, and which Claude model(s) will be enabled for the Taikun agent?
open_decisions.recommended_default: Use a TEEP-governed Bedrock region in us-east-1 if available to minimize latency vs the Taikun us-east-1 processing account; enable the current production Claude model the agent already uses. Do not assume the account/region — confirm with Darko.

open_decisions.question: Are SSO production federation and Bedrock cutover hard prerequisites for starting the 60-day pilot clock, or can the operate window start on the Taikun-managed login + direct-Anthropic LLM path while those gates complete?
open_decisions.recommended_default: Start the pilot clock once the read-system auto-close path is live, using the Taikun-managed login + direct-Anthropic path; treat SSO + Bedrock as blocking for full production cutover only, not for read-system verification — reflecting the honest slip past the deck's 6-8 weeks.

open_decisions.question: What is the data-residency / latency acceptance bound for routing LLM classification through TEEP Bedrock vs the default direct-Anthropic path?
open_decisions.recommended_default: Require decision-parity on 20+ known alerts and a per-classification latency within ~2x the direct path; keep direct-Anthropic as documented rollback if Bedrock latency or availability is unacceptable.

tasks.title: Cutover readiness kickoff + per-system go-live sequencing plan
tasks.description: Run a joint kickoff to agree the per-system go-live order (read systems first: Sensirion, Cygnet, ProCount, Carte; TaskHub write last), the base-URL cutover mechanism (config-only flip from bootstrap S3-drop source to gateway), the go/no-go gates per system, and the email-fallback trigger if TaskHub write slips. Produce a one-page sequencing plan + RACI naming the go-live owner per slice (Michelle/Sebastian for Sensirion+TaskHub, Mike for Cygnet, IFS Merrick owner for ProCount/Carte, Darko for gateway, Sahir for SSO).
tasks.owner_person_or_role: Taikun delivery lead + Darko (TEEP gateway) + Steve
tasks.entry_criteria: Project Maxwell Phase-1 kickoff complete; the five-system list and TaskHub write scope confirmed (PRD Q1, Q3).
tasks.exit_criteria: Sequencing plan + RACI signed off by Darko and Taikun; go-live order, base-URL flip mechanism, and email-fallback trigger documented.
tasks.deliverable: Per-system go-live sequencing plan + RACI (one page) with go/no-go gates

tasks.title: Stand up the gateway-pointed test harness on the AWS test VM
tasks.description: Take the existing api-tests/ harness (run-tests.sh: lint -> Prism mock -> smoke.sh 17 endpoints -> Schemathesis -> simulator.py 4 scenarios) on test VM i-02c3ebb814234cb3b and parameterize --base-url and the OAuth2 bearer so the identical suite can target (a) the Prism mock, (b) bootstrap S3-drop data, and (c) TEEP's real gateway as endpoints land. Add a CI-style summary that records pass/fail per endpoint per target. No spec changes — this is the cutover-verification rig the brief promises ('same code, different --base-url').
tasks.entry_criteria: teep-api.yaml is the frozen contract for Phase 1; test VM access available; api-tests/ suite green against Prism mock (already proven 2026-05-19).
tasks.exit_criteria: run-tests.sh runs against any base URL with injected OAuth2 token; produces per-endpoint per-target pass/fail summary; green against mock as the baseline.
tasks.deliverable: Parameterized gateway-verification harness (run-tests.sh + smoke.sh + simulator.py) with per-target result reports

tasks.title: Smoke + simulator dry-run against bootstrap S3-drop data
tasks.description: Before any gateway exists, point the harness at the bootstrap S3 JSON drops (shaped exactly like the future REST responses, per the brief) for all four read systems plus the Sierra-xlsx Sensirion 'bootstrap-0'. Confirm the agent makes the right calls in the right order and the auto-close/dispatch decision logic fires correctly on real-shaped data. This validates the config-only cutover claim: if it passes on S3-drop shapes it will pass on the gateway with only a base-URL change. Surface any shape drift between the S3 drops and teep-api.yaml back to the bootstrap/asset-registry workstream.
tasks.entry_criteria: Bootstrap S3 drops for at least Sensirion + one read system available and signed (HMAC) per the bootstrap workstream; harness parameterized (CUTOVER-2).
tasks.exit_criteria: Simulator's 4 scenarios pass against S3-drop data; any JSON-shape drift logged as a defect to the bootstrap workstream; auto-close vs dispatch decisions verified against known Jan-2026 alerts.
tasks.deliverable: Bootstrap dry-run report (per-scenario pass/fail + shape-drift defect list)

tasks.title: Production monitoring wiring — Prometheus to CloudWatch + Integration Health screen
tasks.description: Wire the production observability the success criteria depend on: emit and ship teep_api_calls_total{system,endpoint,status}, teep_api_latency_ms, teep_api_errors_total{system,reason} plus the FR-35 agent KPIs (events/day, classification-mix, MTTA, MTTR, auto-close-rate, dispatch-to-close time, TaskHub open->close latency) from Prometheus to CloudWatch in Taikun's us-east-1 account. Light up the existing Integration Health screen (mockups-html/04-integration-health.html) against live metrics so per-system up/down/latency is visible to Darko and Mike. Confirm logs remain metadata-only (endpoint/status/latency/hash — no raw bodies) per the residency commitment.
tasks.entry_criteria: Metric names defined in PRD (FR-32, FR-35); CloudWatch target account confirmed (Taikun us-east-1).
tasks.exit_criteria: Prometheus metrics flowing to CloudWatch; Integration Health screen shows live per-system status/latency; metadata-only logging verified (no raw bodies); a synthetic failure raises the per-system error metric.
tasks.deliverable: Live monitoring stack (Prometheus->CloudWatch) + functioning Integration Health screen

tasks.title: Failure-notification channel wiring (email default / Teams / PagerDuty)
tasks.description: Wire the alerting the brief and security-answers doc specify: threshold of >=5 failures within 5 min for the same system raises an alert to the designated TEEP contact; auth failures (401/403) rotate-retry-once-then-alert; >15-min system-unavailable triggers degraded mode (flag in-flight events Undetected, notify MRO that automated triage is degraded, keep Sierra's dashboard up, never silently drop or invent a classification). Default channel is email; wire Teams or PagerDuty/Opsgenie if TEEP provides an integration key. Requires Darko to name the contact + channel (OD-2).
tasks.entry_criteria: Notification contact + channel decided by Darko (OD-2); monitoring metrics live (CUTOVER-11).
tasks.exit_criteria: Synthetic >=5-in-5-min failure raises an alert on the chosen channel to the named contact; degraded-mode (>15min) behavior demonstrated (events flagged Undetected, MRO notified, dashboard stays up); auth-failure rotate-retry-alert verified.
tasks.deliverable: Failure-notification runbook + verified alert delivery on chosen channel

tasks.title: Per-system contract conformance test as each gateway endpoint lands
tasks.description: As TEEP's gateway exposes each system's endpoints, run the smoke subset for that system against the real gateway base URL with real OAuth2 client-credentials. Verify status codes, content-type, RFC7807 problem+json on errors (the one thing Prism could not reproduce), Idempotency-Key handling on writes, and HMAC webhook signature verification (X-TEEP-Signature + X-TEEP-Timestamp, +/-5min skew). Run once per system (Sensirion, Cygnet-via-intermediary, ProCount, Carte, TaskHub-read, TaskHub-write). File any conformance gaps as gateway defects to the gateway workstream (GW-*).
tasks.owner_person_or_role: Taikun eng (runs) + Darko (gateway fixes) + per-system owner
tasks.entry_criteria: A given system's gateway endpoints deployed (depends on GW-* + the per-system integration workstreams); OAuth2 client-credentials issued to Taikun.
tasks.exit_criteria: Each landed system passes its smoke subset against the real gateway including RFC7807 error shape, idempotency, and (where applicable) HMAC webhook verification; conformance defects filed and triaged.
tasks.deliverable: Per-system gateway conformance reports + defect log against GW-*

tasks.title: Email-fallback path verification (TaskHub-write-slips contingency)
tasks.description: Implement-and-verify the brief's safety valve: if the TaskHub write API is not ready by go-live, the agent emails the MRO team the full evidence pack instead of POSTing a TaskHub task, config-toggled per environment. Verify the email-fallback produces the same evidence pack, that the office-cleared auto-close path is fully unaffected (it needs no write), and that toggling back to TaskHub-write is a config flip. This lets the read-system pilot and the 56.5% office-cleared value start on time even if write slips.
tasks.owner_person_or_role: Taikun eng + Devin (confirms MRO email is actionable)
tasks.entry_criteria: Decision on whether TaskHub write is ready by go-live pending (OD-1); evidence-pack format finalized.
tasks.exit_criteria: Email-fallback sends a complete evidence pack to MRO on a dispatch decision; office-cleared path proven independent of write; config toggle between email-fallback and TaskHub-write verified both directions.
tasks.deliverable: Email-fallback verification report + per-environment config toggle

tasks.title: SSO production-cutover gate verification (TEEP users into the Taikun app)
tasks.description: Verify, jointly with Sahir/TEEP IT, that TEEP users can sign into the Taikun platform with corporate Entra ID credentials before declaring full production cutover: enterprise app registration in the TEEP Entra tenant, redirect/reply URLs, claims + group->role mapping (Devin/MRO, Sierra/HSE-reporting, Clovis/ops, Darko-Mike/integration-health), optional SCIM provisioning, and conditional-access review passed. This workstream consumes the SSO build (SSO-* / identity workstream) and gates production user access — until it passes, the pilot dashboards are accessed via the Taikun-managed login on the demo VM, not TEEP corporate identity.
tasks.entry_criteria: Entra enterprise app registered + claims/group mapping configured by the identity workstream; conditional-access review scheduled with Sahir.
tasks.exit_criteria: Devin, Sierra, Clovis each sign in via Entra ID and land on their role-mapped view; conditional-access review passed; SCIM (if in scope) provisions/deprovisions a test user.
tasks.deliverable: SSO production-cutover sign-off (role-mapped login verified for each persona)

tasks.title: Bedrock production-LLM cutover verification (latency + data-residency)
tasks.description: Verify the agent's LLM inference runs through TEEP's AWS Bedrock (replacing the default direct-Anthropic path for production) before the auto-close/classification path is declared production-ready: cross-account IAM access working, required Claude model(s) enabled in the TEEP Bedrock region, the Taikun LiteLLM gateway pointed at Bedrock, and end-to-end classification of >=20 known Jan-2026 alerts produces the same decisions as the validated path within acceptable latency. Confirm data-residency (TEEP-governed Bedrock for LLM calls). Consumes the Bedrock build (BEDROCK-* workstream). Gates the auto-close/classification go-live because every classification is an LLM call.
tasks.entry_criteria: TEEP AWS account + Bedrock region known (OD-5); cross-account IAM role + Claude model enablement done by the Bedrock workstream; LiteLLM gateway repointed.
tasks.exit_criteria: 20+ known alerts classify through Bedrock with decision-parity vs the validated path; latency within agreed bound; data-residency confirmed; fallback-to-direct-Anthropic path documented as rollback.
tasks.deliverable: Bedrock production-LLM cutover sign-off (decision-parity + latency + residency verified)

tasks.title: Full end-to-end production rehearsal (all systems, all 4 scenarios)
tasks.description: With read systems cut over to the gateway (CUTOVER-8), TaskHub write live or email-fallback armed (CUTOVER-9/10), SSO (CUTOVER-13) and Bedrock (CUTOVER-14) gates passed, and monitoring+alerting live (CUTOVER-11/12): run a full production rehearsal of all four scenarios (auto-close, dispatch, monitor, timeout) against the real stack using replayed Jan-2026 events. This is the single go/no-go rehearsal before declaring production. Confirm the complete close-the-loop: detect -> 4 parallel reads -> classify (via Bedrock) -> act -> monitor -> close -> Sierra's 22 columns populated with zero transcription.
tasks.owner_person_or_role: Taikun eng (drives) + Darko + Michelle + Mike + Sebastian + Sierra
tasks.entry_criteria: All read systems cut over; write live or email-fallback armed; SSO + Bedrock gates passed; monitoring + alerting live.
tasks.exit_criteria: All 4 scenarios pass end-to-end against the real stack on replayed events; Sierra confirms her 22 columns auto-populate; go/no-go decision recorded as GO for production.
tasks.deliverable: Production-rehearsal report + signed go/no-go decision

tasks.title: Sensirion go-live slice — live webhook ingestion verification
tasks.description: Verify the latency-critical path: TEEP/Nubo POSTs a signed Sensirion event to Taikun's webhook endpoint, the HMAC signature validates within the +/-5min skew window, and the agent triggers triage in seconds (the brief's ~4-sec target, replacing the 5.2h email delay). Replay a known Jan-2026 event end-to-end and confirm emissions.alerts INSERT with status=Open + email_received timestamp. Confirm the GET poll fallback works if a webhook is missed. Depends on the Sensirion integration workstream pinning device poll cadence and device->pad/well mapping.
tasks.entry_criteria: Sensirion API integrated into Taikun platform; poll cadence confirmed; device->pad/well mapping delivered (Sensirion workstream); webhook HMAC keys exchanged.
tasks.exit_criteria: A live (or replayed) Sensirion event triggers triage in <10s with valid HMAC; poll fallback verified; MTTA measurement starts emitting; alert row written correctly.
tasks.deliverable: Sensirion go-live sign-off (webhook + poll fallback verified, MTTA instrumented)

tasks.title: Cygnet-via-existing-intermediary go-live slice — SCADA signal validation
tasks.description: TEEP does NOT permit a direct CygNet API. With Mike + Darko, confirm the chosen intermediary pipe (FMP/TaskHub ~30-min device poll, ProCount's documented Cygnet integration, or a historian/read-replica/curated export) and validate the adapted Cygnet data contract delivers tubing/line/casing pressure, sales rate, and compressor metrics plus the 4h pre-event window. Spot-check against the 95/168 alerts that cite Cygnet signals: confirm the pressure-drop signatures the auto-close logic depends on are present and correct in the intermediary feed. Note: the intermediary's coarser cadence (e.g. ~30 min) may blunt the 4h-pre-event-window fidelity — record actual achievable resolution.
tasks.entry_criteria: Intermediary path decided (open decision OD-3); Cygnet adapter adapted to that pipe by the Cygnet workstream; endpoint live on gateway or intermediary.
tasks.exit_criteria: Cygnet signals validated against >=10 known Jan-2026 alerts; pressure-drop signatures present at usable resolution; achievable pre-event-window cadence documented; any KPI impact from coarse cadence flagged.
tasks.deliverable: Cygnet-via-intermediary go-live sign-off + signal-fidelity note (actual cadence vs 4h-window need)

tasks.title: ProCount + Carte go-live slice — codes/comments/injection read validation
tasks.description: Validate ProCount down/up codes, operator comments, and work orders (56/168 alerts) and Carte injection-rate series (22/168, optional) against the real gateway. Confirm whether Carte's injection data is satisfiable through the ProCount API (per the open question to the IFS Merrick owner) — if so, drop the separate Carte endpoint from the go-live checklist. Spot-check codes/comments against known alerts to confirm the evidence pack the agent assembles matches what Devin reads manually today.
tasks.owner_person_or_role: IFS Merrick stack owner (TBD) + Taikun eng
tasks.entry_criteria: IFS Merrick stack owner identified (OD-4 / cross-workstream); ProCount (and Carte unless folded in) endpoints live on gateway.
tasks.exit_criteria: ProCount codes/comments/work-orders validated against >=10 known alerts; Carte-via-ProCount question resolved (Carte dropped or kept); evidence-pack parity with Devin's manual lookup confirmed.
tasks.deliverable: ProCount/Carte go-live sign-off + Carte-fold-in decision record

tasks.title: Bootstrap-to-gateway base-URL cutover per read system (config-only)
tasks.description: For each read system that passes its conformance slice (CUTOVER-5/6/7), flip Maxwell's data source from the bootstrap S3-drop reader to the gateway base URL via configuration only — no code change, per the brief's 'identical JSON shape means cutover is config-only' guarantee. Do this one system at a time with the prior bootstrap source retained as instant rollback. Re-run that system's smoke + the relevant simulator scenario immediately after each flip to confirm parity.
tasks.entry_criteria: A system has passed its go-live conformance slice; bootstrap source still available for rollback.
tasks.exit_criteria: Each read system serving from the gateway base URL via config; post-flip smoke + simulator scenario green; rollback path (revert config to S3 source) verified for each.
tasks.deliverable: Config-cutover changelog per read system + post-flip parity reports

tasks.title: TaskHub write go-live — idempotency + duplicate-dispatch soak
tasks.description: The single highest-consequence cutover: a duplicate POST /v1/fmp/tasks creates a duplicate real field-dispatch task in front of a lease operator. Before enabling write in production, soak-test against the real TaskHub write API: (1) POST create with Idempotency-Key, then replay the same key on a simulated 5xx retry and confirm TEEP returns the original task (no duplicate) within the 24h replay window; (2) PATCH add-note and PATCH close idempotency; (3) the task.updated inbound webhook round-trip (POST -> LO updates -> webhook in -> agent reads final notes -> PATCH closed). Run the dispatch, monitor, and timeout simulator scenarios against the live write API. Coordinate with Sebastian (builds the API) and Brent (confirms the dispatch task content matches LO expectations).
tasks.owner_person_or_role: Sebastian (TEEP, builds API) + Taikun eng + Brent (HSE field lead)
tasks.entry_criteria: TaskHub read+write API + task.updated webhook deployed (FMP/TaskHub workstream); read slices live; idempotency replay store live on the gateway.
tasks.exit_criteria: Idempotency replay produces zero duplicate tasks across 50+ replayed writes; dispatch/monitor/timeout scenarios pass against live write API; task.updated webhook round-trip verified; Brent confirms dispatch task content is field-actionable.
tasks.deliverable: TaskHub write go-live soak report (zero-duplicate evidence + webhook round-trip proof)

tasks.title: Production go-live + start of 60-day operate window
tasks.description: Declare production: live Sensirion events trigger Maxwell end-to-end, the office-cleared auto-close path runs without human touch, dispatch tasks flow to TaskHub (or email-fallback), and the 60-day pilot clock starts. Establish the operate cadence: daily Integration Health glance, on-call ownership for the failure-notification channel, and the KPI dashboard tracking MTTA, auto-close rate, classification accuracy, and per-system API error rate against the success criteria.
tasks.owner_person_or_role: Taikun eng (operate) + Darko (gateway ops) + Clovis (ops oversight)
tasks.entry_criteria: Production rehearsal GO recorded (CUTOVER-15).
tasks.exit_criteria: Live events processing end-to-end in production; 60-day clock started with a recorded start date; KPI dashboard live; on-call ownership assigned.
tasks.deliverable: Production go-live record + operate runbook + 60-day clock start

tasks.title: Weekly mapping-table review with Sierra (operate window)
tasks.description: Per PRD risk mitigation (HSE/EPA category drift) and FR-29, run a weekly review with Sierra of the free-text-to-category mapping table (LO note -> resolution_type / equipment / equipment_component / epa_identifier) during the operate window. Capture new LO phrasings, adjust the mapping config (not code — columns are configuration), and re-validate that the monthly Excel export still matches her exact 22-column template. This keeps classification accuracy on track toward the >=92% Phase-1 target and ensures her report stays a drop-in replacement.
tasks.entry_criteria: Production live; at least one week of real triage decisions recorded.
tasks.exit_criteria: Weekly review held for the full operate window; mapping-table changes logged and applied as config; monthly export confirmed matching Sierra's template each cycle.
tasks.deliverable: Weekly mapping-review log + versioned mapping-table config history

tasks.title: Operate-window monitoring + incident handling against success criteria
tasks.description: Run the 60-day operate window watching the success criteria live: >=95% Sensirion events ingested without human intervention, median MTTA <=15 min, >=40% auto-closed, Sierra's report generated entirely from the agent store, and zero API incidents forcing Darko to roll back access. Triage any per-system degradation via the notification channel + degraded-mode behavior; log every API incident and its resolution. Capture the operating data that, with the historical pull, firms the KPI commitments (the 22-day sample was too short).
tasks.entry_criteria: Production live; monitoring + notifications verified (CUTOVER-11/12).
tasks.exit_criteria: 60 days of traffic processed; KPI trend reports produced; incident log shows no roll-back-forcing API incident (or documents any that occurred + fix); accumulated data ready for KPI recalibration.
tasks.deliverable: 60-day operate report (KPI trends + incident log) measured against the 6 success criteria

tasks.title: Pilot review + Phase-2 go/no-go decision
tasks.description: At the end of the 60-day window, run the pilot review with Clovis, Steve, Darko, Sierra, and Devin against the 6 success criteria. Secure the written confirmation from Devin and Sierra that they would not return to the manual process (criterion 6). Present KPI actuals vs targets, recommend whether to recalibrate the auto-close target from the Phase-1 40% toward the Phase-2 65% using the accumulated operate-window + historical data, and produce the Phase-2 proposal (operator response handler, LLM free-text reconciliation when rule confidence <0.7, SMS/Teams escalation channels, pre-event Cygnet anomaly detection).
tasks.entry_criteria: 60-day operate report complete (CUTOVER-18); weekly mapping reviews complete (CUTOVER-17).
tasks.exit_criteria: Pilot review held; all 6 success criteria assessed with evidence; Devin + Sierra written sign-off captured; Phase-2 go/no-go recorded with a scoped Phase-2 proposal.
tasks.deliverable: Pilot review readout + Phase-2 go/no-go decision + Phase-2 scope proposal
